1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: QAF. I CALL to witness the glorious Qur'an.

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: Qaf; by oath of the Glorious Qur’an.

1.author: Daryabadi
1.text: Qaf. By the Qur'an glorious, We have sent thee as a warner.

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: Qaf. [These letters (Qaf, etc.) are one of the miracles of the Quran, and none but Allah (Alone) knows their meanings]. By the Glorious Quran.

1.author: Sarwar
1.text: By Qaf and the glorious Quran, (you are the Messenger of God).

1.author: Shakir
1.text: Qaf. I swear by the glorious Quran (that Muhammad is the Apostle of Allah)

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: Qaf: By the Glorious Qur'an (Thou art Allah's Messenger).

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: They are rather surprised that a warner from among themselves has come to them. So the unbelievers say: "This is astonishing:

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: Rather they were shocked that a Herald of Warning came to them from among themselves, so the disbelievers said, “This is something really strange.”

2.author: Arberry
2.text: Nay, but they marvel that a warner has come to them from among them; and the unbelievers say, 'This is a marvellous thing!

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: Aye! they marvel that there hath come Unto them a warner from among themselves; so the infidels Say: this is a thing wondrous!

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: Nay, they wonder that there has come to them a warner (Muhammad SAW) from among themselves. So the disbelievers say: "This is a strange thing!

2.author: Itani
2.text: They marveled that a warner has come to them from among them. The disbelievers say, “This is something strange.

2.author: Maududi
2.text: Nay; they wondered that a warner should have come to them from among themselves. The unbelievers said: “This indeed is a strange thing.

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: Nay, they wonder that there has come to them a warner from among themselves. So the disbelievers say: "This is a strange thing!"

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: Nay, but they marvel that a warner of their own hath come unto them; and the disbelievers say: This is a strange thing:

2.author: Qarai
2.text: Indeed they consider it odd that a warner from among themselves should have come to them. So the faithless say, ‘This is an odd thing.’

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: No, but they marvel that from among themselves a warner has come to them. The unbelievers say (in mockery): 'This is a marvelous thing!

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: But they wonder that there has come to them a warner from among themselves, and the disbelievers say, "This is an amazing thing.

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: In fact, it seems odd (to the pagans) that a warner from their own people has come to them. The disbelievers have said, "It is very strange

2.author: Shakir
2.text: Nay! they wonder that there has come to them a warner from among themselves, so the unbelievers say: This is a wonderful thing:

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: Indeed, they are astonished that a warner should have come to them from among themselves. So these deniers of the truth say, "This is indeed a strange thing,

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: But they wonder that there has come to them a Warner from among themselves. So the Unbelievers say: "This is a wonderful thing!

2.author: Transliteration
2.text: Bal AAajiboo an jaahum munthirun minhum faqala alkafiroona hatha shayon AAajeebun

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: When we are dead and turned to dust, this returning (to life) is most far-fetched."

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: “When we are dead and have turned to dust, will we be raised again? That return is impossible!”

3.author: Arberry
3.text: What, when we are dead and become dust? That is a far returning!'

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: Shell We be brought back when we are dead and have become dust? That is a return remote!

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: "When we are dead and have become dust (shall we be resurrected?) That is a far return."

3.author: Itani
3.text: When we have died and become dust? This is a farfetched return.”

3.author: Maududi
3.text: What! When we are dead and reduced to mere dust, (shall we be raised to life)? Such a return is far-fetched.”

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: "When we are dead and have become dust. (Shall we be resurrected) That is a far return."

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: When we are dead and have become dust (shall we be brought back again)? That would be a far return!

3.author: Qarai
3.text: ‘What! When we are dead and have become dust, [shall we be raised again]? That is a far-fetched return!’

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: What, when we are dead and turned to dust? That indeed would be an unlikely return'

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: When we have died and have become dust, [we will return to life]? That is a distant return."

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: that after we die and become dust, we shall be brought back to life again. This seems far from reality".

3.author: Shakir
3.text: What! when we are dead and have become dust? That is afar (from probable) return.

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: to come back to life after we have died and become dust? That is most improbable!"

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: "What! When we die and become dust, (shall we live again?) That is a (sort of) return far (from our understanding)."

3.author: Transliteration
3.text: Aitha mitna wakunna turaban thalika rajAAun baAAeedun

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: We know what the earth consumes of them, for We have the Book that preserves everything.

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: We know all what the earth decreases from them; and with Us is a Book that retains.

4.author: Arberry
4.text: We know what the earth diminishes of them; with Us is a book recording.

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: Surely We know that which the earth consumeth of them, and with Us is, Book preserved.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: We know that which the earth takes of them (their dead bodies), and with Us is a Book preserved (i.e. the Book of Decrees).

4.author: Itani
4.text: We know what the earth consumes of them, and with Us is a comprehensive book.

4.author: Maududi
4.text: (Thus do they imagine, although) We know well what the earth takes away from them. With Us is a Record that preserves everything.

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: We know that which the earth takes of them, and with Us is a Book preserved.

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: We know that which the earth taketh of them, and with Us is a recording Book.

4.author: Qarai
4.text: We know what the earth diminishes from them, and with Us is a preserving Book.

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: We know all that the earth takes away of them, and a Book of Records is with Us.

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: We know what the earth diminishes of them, and with Us is a retaining record.

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: We already know how much of them (of their bodies) the earth will consume. With Us there is a Book that contains all records.

4.author: Shakir
4.text: We know indeed what the earth diminishes of them, and with Us is a writing that preserves.

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: We know very well what the earth takes away from them: We hold a book which records all things.

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: We already know how much of them the earth takes away: With Us is a record guarding (the full account).

4.author: Transliteration
4.text: Qad AAalimna ma tanqusu alardu minhum waAAindana kitabun hafeethun

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: But no! They called the truth a lie when it came to them; so they are in a confused state.

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: In fact they denied the Truth when it came to them, so they are now in a dilemma.

5.author: Arberry
5.text: Nay, but they cried lies to the truth when it came to them, and so they are in a case confused.

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: Aye! they belie the truth when it cometh Unto them; wherefore they are in an affair confused.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: Nay, but they have denied the truth (this Quran) when it has come to them, so they are in a confused state (can not differentiate between right and wrong).

5.author: Itani
5.text: But they denied the truth when it has come to them, so they are in a confused state.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: They gave the lie to the Truth when it came to them. So they are now in a state of great perplexity.

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: Nay, but they have denied the truth when it has come to them, so they are in a Marij state.

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: Nay, but they have denied the truth when it came unto them, therefor they are now in troubled case.

5.author: Qarai
5.text: Indeed, they denied the truth when it came to them; so they are now in a perplexed state of affairs.

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: Rather, they belied the truth when it came to them, and now they are in a state of confusion.

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: But they denied the truth when it came to them, so they are in a confused condition.

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: In fact, they have rejected the truth that has come to them, thus, they live in confusion.

5.author: Shakir
5.text: Nay, they rejected the truth when it came to them, so they are (now) in a state of confusion.

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: But they denied the truth when it came to them, so they are in a state of confusion.

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: But they deny the Truth when it comes to them: so they are in a confused state.

5.author: Transliteration
5.text: Bal kaththaboo bialhaqqi lamma jaahum fahum fee amrin mareejin

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: Have they not looked at the sky above them, how We have fashioned and adorned it, and it has no flaw?

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: So did they not see the sky above them – how We have made it and beautified it, and there are no cracks in it?

6.author: Arberry
6.text: What, have they not beheld heaven above them, how We have built it, and decked it out fair, and it has no cracks?

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: Have they not looked up to the heaven above them, in what wise We have constructed and bedecked it, and that therein is no rift?

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: Have they not looked at the heaven above them, how We have made it and adorned it, and there are no rifts in it?

6.author: Itani
6.text: Have they not observed the sky above them, how We constructed it, and decorated it, and it has no cracks?

6.author: Maududi
6.text: Did they never observe the sky above them: how We built it and beautified it; and it has no cracks;

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: Have they not looked at the heaven above them, how We have made it and adorned it, and there are no Furuj in it

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: Have they not then observed the sky above them, how We have constructed it and beautified it, and how there are no rifts therein?

6.author: Qarai
6.text: Have they not, then, observed the heaven above them, how We have built it and adorned it, and that there are no cracks in it?

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: What, have they never observed the heaven above them, (and seen) how We built and adorned it, leaving no crack?

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: Have they not looked at the heaven above them - how We structured it and adorned it and [how] it has no rifts?

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: Have they not seen how We have established the sky above them and decked it without gaps and cracks?

6.author: Shakir
6.text: Do they not then look up to heaven above them how We have made it and adorned it and it has no gaps?

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: Have they not observed the sky above them and marked how We have built it and adorned it, leaving no flaws in it;

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: Do they not look at the sky above them?- How We have made it and adorned it, and there are no flaws in it?

6.author: Transliteration
6.text: Afalam yanthuroo ila alssamai fawqahum kayfa banaynaha wazayyannaha wama laha min furoojin

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: We stretched the earth and placed upon it firm stabilisers and We made every kind of splendid thing to grow upon it

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: And (how) We have spread the earth, and placed mountains as anchors in it, and have grown all kinds of beautiful pairs in it?

7.author: Arberry
7.text: And the earth -- We stretched it forth, and cast on it firm mountains, and We caused to grow therein of every joyous kind

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: And the earth! We have spread it forth, and have cast therein firm mountains, and have caused to grow therein of every beauteous kind of plants:

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: And the earth! We have spread it out, and set thereon mountains standing firm, and have produced therein every kind of lovely growth (plants).

7.author: Itani
7.text: And the earth, how We spread it out, and set on it mountains, and grew in it all kinds of delightful pairs?

7.author: Maududi
7.text: and We have spread out the earth, and have set upon it firm mountains, and have caused it to bring out plants of all beauteous kinds?

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: And the earth! We have spread it out, and set thereon Rawasi standing firm, and We planted in it every lovely (Bahij) pair.

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: And the earth have We spread out, and have flung firm hills therein, and have caused of every lovely kind to grow thereon,

7.author: Qarai
7.text: And We spread out the earth, and cast in it firm mountains, and caused every delightful kind [of vegetation] to grow in it.

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: We spread out the earth and set upon it firm mountains, and We cause every delightful thing to grow in it

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: And the earth - We spread it out and cast therein firmly set mountains and made grow therein [something] of every beautiful kind,

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: (Have they not seen) how We have spread out the earth, placed on it firm mountains and have made all kinds of flourishing pairs of plants grow?

7.author: Shakir
7.text: And the earth, We have made it plain and cast in it mountains and We have made to grow therein of all beautiful kinds,

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: We spread out the earth and set upon it solid mountains and We brought forth from it all kinds of delightful plants,

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: And the earth- We have spread it out, and set thereon mountains standing firm, and produced therein every kind of beautiful growth (in pairs)-

7.author: Transliteration
7.text: Waalarda madadnaha waalqayna feeha rawasiya waanbatna feeha min kulli zawjin baheejin

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: As instruction and reminder to every penitent creature.

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: A perception and an understanding, for every bondman who inclines.

8.author: Arberry
8.text: for an insight and a reminder to every penitent servant.

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: An insight and an admonition for every bondman penitent.

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: An insight and a Reminder for every slave turning to Allah (i.e. the one who believes in Allah and performs deeds of His obedience, and always begs His pardon).

8.author: Itani
8.text: A lesson and a reminder for every penitent worshiper.

8.author: Maududi
8.text: All these are to serve as eye openers and as a lesson to every being who is prone to turn (to the Truth).

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: An insight and a Reminder for every servant who turns to Allah in repentance.

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: A vision and a reminder for every penitent slave.

8.author: Qarai
8.text: [In this there is] an insight and admonition for every penitent servant.

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: as a lesson and a reminder to every penitent worshiper.

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: Giving insight and a reminder for every servant who turns [to Allah].

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: This is a reminder and it sharpens the insight of every servant of God who turns to Him in repentance.

8.author: Shakir
8.text: To give sight and as a reminder to every servant who turns frequently (to Allah).

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: as a lesson and reminder for every human being who turns to God;

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: To be observed and commemorated by every devotee turning (to Allah).

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: And We send down water as a blessing from the sky, and grow gardens with it and the grain for harvest,

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: And We sent down the auspicious water from the sky, therefore producing gardens with it, and the grain that is harvested.

9.author: Arberry
9.text: And We sent down out of heaven water blessed, and caused to grow thereby gardens and grain of harvest

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: And We have sent down from the heaven water brest wherewith We have caused gardens to grow and the grain reaped.

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: And We send down blessed water (rain) from the sky, then We produce therewith gardens and grain (every kind of harvests) that are reaped.

9.author: Itani
9.text: And We brought down from the sky blessed water, and produced with it gardens and grain to harvest.

9.author: Maududi
9.text: We also sent down blessed water from the heaven, wherewith We caused gardens and harvest-grain to grow,

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: And We send down blessed water (rain) from the sky, then We produce therewith Jannat and grain that are reaped.

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: And We send down from the sky blessed water whereby We give growth unto gardens and the grain of crops,

9.author: Qarai
9.text: We send down from the sky salubrious water, with which We grow gardens and the grain which is harvested,

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: We sent down blessed water from the sky with which We caused gardens and the grains of harvest to grow,

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: And We have sent down blessed rain from the sky and made grow thereby gardens and grain from the harvest

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: We have sent blessed water down from the sky to grow gardens, harvestable crops

9.author: Shakir
9.text: And We send down from the cloud water abounding in good, then We cause to grow thereby gardens and the grain that is reaped,

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: and We have sent down from the sky blessed water with which We have brought forth gardens and grain to be harvested,

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: And We send down from the sky rain charted with blessing, and We produce therewith gardens and Grain for harvests;

9.author: Transliteration
9.text: Wanazzalna mina alssamai maan mubarakan faanbatna bihi jannatin wahabba alhaseedi

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: And tall date-palms with their spathes pile on pile

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: And high date-palms with lush fruit.

10.author: Arberry
10.text: and tall palm-trees with spathes compact,

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: And tall date-palms laden with clusters ranged.

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: And tall date-palms, with ranged clusters;

10.author: Itani
10.text: And the soaring palm trees, with clustered dates.

10.author: Maududi
10.text: and tall palm-trees with their thickly-clustered spathes;

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: And date palms Basiqat, with arranged clusters.

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: And lofty date-palms with ranged clusters,

10.author: Qarai
10.text: and tall date palms with regularly set spathes,

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: and tall palmtrees with compact spathes

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: And lofty palm trees having fruit arranged in layers -

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: and tall palm-trees with clusters of dates

10.author: Shakir
10.text: And the tall palm-trees having spadices closely set one above another,

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: and tall palm-trees with their thickly-clustered dates,

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: And tall (and stately) palm-trees, with shoots of fruit-stalks, piled one over another;-

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: As a provision for men, and enlivened a region that was dead. Even thus will be the life after death.

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: As sustenance for the bondmen; and with it We revived a dead city; this is how you will be raised.

11.author: Arberry
11.text: a provision for the servants, and thereby We revived a land that was dead. Even so is the coming forth.

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: As a provision for our bondmen; and therewith We have quickened a dead land. Even so will be the coming forth.

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: A provision for (Allah's) slaves. And We give life therewith to a dead land. Thus will be the resurrection (of the dead).

11.author: Itani
11.text: As sustenance for the servants. And We revive thereby a dead town. Likewise is the resurrection.

11.author: Maududi
11.text: all this as sustenance for Our servants. And thus We do bring the dead land back to life with water. Such shall be the coming forth (of human beings from the earth).

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: A provision for the servants. And We give life therewith to a dead land. Thus will be the Resurrection.

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: Provision (made) for men; and therewith We quicken a dead land. Even so will be the resurrection of the dead.

11.author: Qarai
11.text: as a provision for Our servants, and with it We revive a dead country. Likewise will be the rising [from the dead].

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: as a provision for the worshipers; thereby We revived a land that was dead. Such shall be the emerging.

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: As provision for the servants, and We have given life thereby to a dead land. Thus is the resurrection.

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: as sustenance for My servants. With this We have brought the dead land back to life. Thus, will also be your resurrection.

11.author: Shakir
11.text: A sustenance for the servants, and We give life thereby to a dead land; thus is the rising.

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: as a provision for human beings; and by [all] this We bring dead land to life. Such shall be the Resurrection.

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: As sustenance for (Allah's) Servants;- and We give (new) life therewith to land that is dead: Thus will be the Resurrection.

11.author: Transliteration
11.text: Rizqan lilAAibadi waahyayna bihi baldatan maytan kathalika alkhurooju

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: The people of Noah, Ar-Rass and Thamud denied before you,

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: Before these, the people of Nooh had denied, and so did the dwellers of Rass and the Thamud.

12.author: Arberry
12.text: Cried lies before them the people of Noah and the men of Er-Rass, and Thamood,

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: And before them the people of Nuh belied, and so did the dwellers Of Rass and the Thamud.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: Denied before them (i.e. these pagans of Makkah who denied you, O Muhammad SAW) the people of Nuh (Noah), and the dwellers of Rass, and the Thamud,

12.author: Itani
12.text: Before them the people of Noah denied the truth, and so did the dwellers of Russ, and Thamood.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: In the past Noah's people, and the people of Rass and Thamud gave the lie (to Messengers),

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: Denied before them the people of Nuh, and the Dwellers of Rass, and Thamud,

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: The folk of Noah denied (the truth) before them, and (so did) the dwellers at Ar-Rass and (the tribe of) Thamud,

12.author: Qarai
12.text: The people of Noah denied before them, and [so did] the people of Rass and Thamud,

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: Before them, the nation of Noah, the nation of ArRass belied and so did Thamood

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: The people of Noah denied before them, and the companions of the well and Thamud

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: The people of Noah, dwellers of the Ra's, Thamud,

12.author: Shakir
12.text: (Others) before them rejected (prophets): the people of Nuh and the dwellers of Ar-Rass and Samood,

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: Before them, the people of Noah and the people of Rass denied this truth; and so did the people of Thamud.

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: Before them was denied (the Hereafter) by the People of Noah, the Companions of the Rass, the Thamud,

12.author: Transliteration
12.text: Kaththabat qablahum qawmu noohin waashabu alrrassi wathamoodu

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: As did the people of 'Ad and Pharaoh and the brethren of Lot,

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: And the tribe of A’ad, and Firaun, and the fellowmen of Lut.

13.author: Arberry
13.text: and Ad and Pharaoh, the brothers of Lot,

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: And the A-ad, and Fir'awn. and the brethren of Lut.

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: And 'Ad, and Fir'aun (Pharaoh), and the brethren of Lout (Lot),

13.author: Itani
13.text: And Aad, and Pharaoh, and the brethren of Lot.

13.author: Maududi
13.text: and so did 'Ad, and Pharaoh and Lot's brethren,

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: And `Ad, and Fir`awn, and the brethren of Lut,

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: And (the tribe of) A'ad, and Pharaoh, and the brethren of Lot,

13.author: Qarai
13.text: and ‘Ad, Pharaoh, and the brethren of Lot,

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: and Aad, Pharaoh and the brothers of Lot,

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: And 'Aad and Pharaoh and the brothers of Lot

13.author: Shakir
13.text: And Ad and Firon and Lut's brethren,

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: And the tribe of 'Ad, and Pharaoh, and the brethren of Lot,

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: The 'Ad, Pharaoh, the brethren of Lut,

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: And the dwellers of the Wood and people of Tubba'. Each of them denied the apostles. So My threat became a reality.

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: And the Dwellers of the Woods, and the people of Tubb’a; each one of them one denied the Noble Messengers, so My promised punishment proved true.

14.author: Arberry
14.text: the men of the Thicket, the people of Tubba'. Every one cried lies to the Messengers, and My threat came true.

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: And the dwellers in the wood, and the people of Tubba. Each one belied the apostles, wherefore fulfilled was My judgement.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: And the dwellers of the Wood, and the people of Tubba'; everyone of them denied (their) Messengers, so My Threat took effect.

14.author: Itani
14.text: And the Dwellers of the Woods, and the people of Tubba. They all rejected the messengers, so My threat came true.

14.author: Maududi
14.text: and the people of Aykah, and the people of Tubba'. They all gave the lie to the Messengers. Thereafter My threat of chastisement against them was fulfilled.

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: And the Dwellers of Al-Aykah, and the people of Tubba`. Everyone of them denied (their) Messengers, so My threat took effect.

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: And the dwellers in the wood, and the folk of Tubb'a: every one denied their messengers, therefor My threat took effect.

14.author: Qarai
14.text: and the inhabitants of Aykah and the people of Tubbac. Each [of them] impugned the apostles, and so My threat became due [against them].

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: the dwellers of the Thicket and the nation of Tubba', all belied their Messengers, therefore My threat was realized.

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: And the companions of the thicket and the people of Tubba'. All denied the messengers, so My threat was justly fulfilled.

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: dwellers of the forest, and the people of Tubba had all rejected the Prophets. Thus, they became subject to Our torment.

14.author: Shakir
14.text: And the dwellers of the grove and the people of Tuba; all rejected the apostles, so My threat came to pass.

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: and the dwellers of the Wood, and the people of Tubba': every one denied their messengers, and so My warning came true.

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: The Companions of the Wood, and the People of Tubba'; each one (of them) rejected the messengers, and My warning was duly fulfilled (in them).

14.author: Transliteration
14.text: Waashabu alaykati waqawmu tubbaAAin kullun kaththaba alrrusula fahaqqa waAAeedi

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: Were We exhausted by the act of the first creation? And yet they are confused about a new creation.

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: So did We tire by creating the first time? Rather they doubt being created again.

15.author: Arberry
15.text: What, were We wearied by the first creation? No indeed; but they are in uncertainty as to the new creation.

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: Are We then wearied with the first creation? Aye! they are in dubiety regarding a new creation.

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: Were We then tired with the first creation? Nay, They are in confused doubt about a new creation (i.e. Resurrection)?

15.author: Itani
15.text: Were We fatigued by the first creation? But they are in doubt of a new creation.

15.author: Maududi
15.text: Did We, then, become worn out by the first creation? Not at all; but they are in doubt about a fresh creation.

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: Were We then tired with the first creation Nay, they are in confused doubt about a new creation.

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: Were We then worn out by the first creation? Yet they are in doubt about a new creation.

15.author: Qarai
15.text: Have We been exhausted by the first creation? No, they are in doubt about a new creation.

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: What, were We wearied by the first creation? No, indeed; yet they are in doubt about a new creation.

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: Did We fail in the first creation? But they are in confusion over a new creation.

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: Did We fail to accomplish the first creation? Of course, We did not; We have all power over all things. Yet they are confused about a new creation.

15.author: Shakir
15.text: Were We then fatigued with the first creation? Yet are they in doubt with regard to a new creation.

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: Were We then worn out by the first creation? Yet they are in doubt about a second creation.

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: Were We then weary with the first Creation, that they should be in confused doubt about a new Creation?

15.author: Transliteration
15.text: AfaAAayeena bialkhalqi alawwali bal hum fee labsin min khalqin jadeedin

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: He created man and surely know what misdoubts arise in their hearts; for We are closer to him than his jugular vein.

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: And indeed We have created man and We know what his soul instils in him; and We are nearer to him than the hearts artery.

16.author: Arberry
16.text: We indeed created man; and We know what his soul whispers within him, and We are nearer to him than the jugular vein.

16.author: Daryabadi
16.text: And assuredly We have created man and We know whatsoever his soul whispereth Unto him, and We are nigher Unto him than his jugular vein.

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: And indeed We have created man, and We know what his ownself whispers to him. And We are nearer to him than his jugular vein (by Our Knowledge).

16.author: Itani
16.text: We created the human being, and We know what his soul whispers to him. We are nearer to him than his jugular vein.

16.author: Maududi
16.text: Surely We have created man, and We know the promptings of his heart, and We are nearer to him than even his jugular vein.

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: And indeed We have created man, and We know what his self whispers to him. And We are nearer to him than his jugular vein.

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: We verily created man and We know what his soul whispereth to him, and We are nearer to him than his jugular vein.

16.author: Qarai
16.text: Certainly We have created man and We know to what his soul tempts him, and We are nearer to him than his jugular vein.

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: Indeed, We created the human. We know the whisperings of his soul, and are closer to him than the jugular vein.

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: And We have already created man and know what his soul whispers to him, and We are closer to him than [his] jugular vein

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: We swear that We have created the human being and We know what his soul whispers to him. We are closer to him than even his jugular vein.

16.author: Shakir
16.text: And certainly We created man, and We know what his mind suggests to him, and We are nearer to him than his life-vein.

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: We created man -- We know the promptings of his soul, and are closer to him than his jugular vein --

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: It was We Who created man, and We know what dark suggestions his soul makes to him: for We are nearer to him than (his) jugular vein.

16.author: Transliteration
16.text: Walaqad khalaqna alinsana wanaAAlamu ma tuwaswisu bihi nafsuhu wanahnu aqrabu ilayhi min habli alwareedi

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: When the two (angels) who keep the account, one sitting on the right, one on the left, take it down,

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: When the two receiving angels receive from him, one seated on the right and one on the left.

17.author: Arberry
17.text: When the two angels meet together, sitting one on the right, and one on the left,

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: Behold! when the two receivers receive-one on the right hand and one on the left a sitter.

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: (Remember!) that the two receivers (recording angels) receive (each human being after he or she has attained the age of puberty), one sitting on the right and one on the left (to note his or her actions).

17.author: Itani
17.text: As the two receivers receive, seated to the right and to the left.

17.author: Maududi
17.text: Moreover, there are two scribes, one each sitting on the right and the left, recording everything.

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: (Remember) that the two receivers (recording angels) receive (each human being), one sitting on the right and one on the left.

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: When the two Receivers receive (him), seated on the right hand and on the left,

17.author: Qarai
17.text: When the twin recorders record [his deeds], seated on the right hand and on the left:

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: When both receivers (the angels) receive, one on his right, the other on his left,

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: When the two receivers receive, seated on the right and on the left.

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: Since the two scribes are sitting on each of his shoulders, he does not utter a word which is not recorded immediately by the watchful scribes.

17.author: Shakir
17.text: When the two receivers receive, sitting on the right and on the left.

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: and the two recording angels are recording, sitting on the right and the left:

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: Behold, two (guardian angels) appointed to learn (his doings) learn (and noted them), one sitting on the right and one on the left.

17.author: Transliteration
17.text: Ith yatalaqqa almutalaqqiyani AAani alyameeni waAAani alshshimali qaAAeedun

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: There is not a word he utters but an observer is ready (to make note of it).

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: He does not utter a single word, without a ready recorder seated next to him.

18.author: Arberry
18.text: not a word he utters, but by him is an observer ready.

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: Not a word he uttereth but there is with him a watcher ready.

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: Not a word does he (or she) utter, but there is a watcher by him ready (to record it).

18.author: Itani
18.text: Not a word does he utter, but there is a watcher by him, ready.

18.author: Maududi
18.text: He utters not a word, but there is a vigilant watcher at hand.

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: Not a word does he (or she) utter but there is a watcher by him ready (to record it).

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: He uttereth no word but there is with him an observer ready.

18.author: Qarai
18.text: he says no word but that there is a ready observer beside him.

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: whatever phrase he utters, an observer is present.

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: Man does not utter any word except that with him is an observer prepared [to record].

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: The human being will certainly experience the agony of death

18.author: Shakir
18.text: He utters not a word but there is by him a watcher at hand.

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: each word he utters shall be noted down by a vigilant guardian.

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: Not a word does he utter but there is a sentinel by him, ready (to note it).

18.author: Transliteration
18.text: Ma yalfithu min qawlin illa ladayhi raqeebun AAateedun

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: The palsy of death will surely come. This is what you wished to avert.

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: And the hardship of death came with the truth; “This is what you were escaping from!”

19.author: Arberry
19.text: And death's agony comes in truth; that is what thou wast shunning!

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: And the stupor of death will come in truth: this is that which thou hast been shunning.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: And the stupor of death will come in truth: "This is what you have been avoiding!"

19.author: Itani
19.text: The daze of death has come in truth: “This is what you tried to evade.”

19.author: Maududi
19.text: Lo, the agony of death has indeed come with the Truth. That is what you had sought to avoid.

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: And the stupor of death will come in truth: "This is what you have been avoiding!"

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: And the agony of death cometh in truth. (And it is said unto him): This is that which thou wast wont to shun.

19.author: Qarai
19.text: The throes of death bring the truth: ‘This is what you used to shun!’

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: And when the agony of death comes in truth (they will say): 'This is what you have been trying to avoid'

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: And the intoxication of death will bring the truth; that is what you were trying to avoid.

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: and (the human being will be told), "This is what you had been trying to run away from".

19.author: Shakir
19.text: And the stupor of death will come in truth; that is what you were trying to escape.

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: The trance of death will come revealing the truth: that is what you were trying to escape.

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: And the stupor of death will bring Truth (before his eyes): "This was the thing which thou wast trying to escape!"

19.author: Transliteration
19.text: Wajaat sakratu almawti bialhaqqi thalika ma kunta minhu taheedu

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: And the trumpet blast will sound: It would be the Day of Doom.

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: And the Trumpet was blown; “This is the Day of the promised punishment.”

20.author: Arberry
20.text: And the Trumpet shall be blown; that is the Day of the Threat.

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: And the trumpet will be blown: this is the Day of the Threatening.

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: And the Trumpet will be blown, that will be the Day whereof warning (had been given) (i.e. the Day of Resurrection).

20.author: Itani
20.text: And the Trumpet is blown: “This is the Promised Day.”

20.author: Maududi
20.text: And then the Trumpet was blown. This is the day of the promised chastisement.

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: And the Trumpet will be blown -- that will be the Day of the threat.

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: And the trumpet is blown. This is the threatened Day.

20.author: Qarai
20.text: And the Trumpet will be blown: ‘This is the promised day.’

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: And the Horn shall be blown; that is the Day of Threat!

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: And the Horn will be blown. That is the Day of [carrying out] the threat.

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: The trumpet will certainly be sounded. This will be the day (about which you) were threatened.

20.author: Shakir
20.text: And the trumpet shall be blown; that is the day of the threatening.

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: The trumpet will be sounded. This is the Day [you were] warned of.

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: And the Trumpet shall be blown: that will be the Day whereof Warning (had been given).

20.author: Transliteration
20.text: Wanufikha fee alssoori thalika yawmu alwaAAeedi

21.author: Ahmed Ali
21.text: Each soul will come with a driver and a witness.

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: And every soul came, along with a herder and a witness.

21.author: Arberry
21.text: And every soul shall come, and with it a driver and a witness.

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: And there shall come every soul therewith shall be a driver and a witness.

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: And every person will come forth along with an (angel) to drive (him), and an (angel) to bear witness.

21.author: Itani
21.text: And every soul will come forward, accompanied by a driver and a witness.

21.author: Maududi
21.text: Everyone has come, each attended by one who will drive him on, and another who will bear witness.

21.author: Mubarakpuri
21.text: And every person will come forth along with a Sa'iq and a Shahid.

21.author: Pickthall
21.text: And every soul cometh, along with it a driver and a witness.

21.author: Qarai
21.text: Every soul will come accompanied by [two angels], a driver, and a witness:

21.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
21.text: Each soul shall come with a driver, and a witness.

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: And every soul will come, with it a driver and a witness.

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: Every soul will be accompanied (by an angel) behind him and another as a witness.

21.author: Shakir
21.text: And every soul shall come, with it a driver and a witness.

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: Each person will arrive attended by an [angel] to drive him on and another to bear witness.

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: And there will come forth every soul: with each will be an (angel) to drive, and an (angel) to bear witness.

21.author: Transliteration
21.text: Wajaat kullu nafsin maAAaha saiqun washaheedun

22.author: Ahmed Ali
22.text: (And the driver will say:) "You were oblivious of this, so we have removed the veil, and how keen is your sight today!"

22.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
22.text: “You were indeed neglectful of this, so We have removed the veil for you, and your eyesight is sharp this day.”

22.author: Arberry
22.text: 'Thou wast heedless of this; therefore We have now removed from thee thy covering, and so thy sight today is piercing.'

22.author: Daryabadi
22.text: Assuredly thou wast in neglect thereof; now We have removed from off thee thy veil, so thy sight Today is piercing.

22.author: Hilali & Khan
22.text: (It will be said to the sinners): "Indeed you were heedless of this, now We have removed your covering, and sharp is your sight this Day!"

22.author: Itani
22.text: “You were in neglect of this, so We lifted your screen from you, and your vision today is keen.”

22.author: Maududi
22.text: You were heedless of this. Now We have removed your veil and so your vision today is sharp.

22.author: Mubarakpuri
22.text: (It will be said to the sinners): "Indeed you were heedless of this. Now We have removed from you, your covering, and sharp is your sight this Day!"

22.author: Pickthall
22.text: (And unto the evil-doer it is said): Thou wast in heedlessness of this. Now We have removed from thee thy covering, and piercing is thy sight this day.

22.author: Qarai
22.text: [he will be told] ‘You were certainly oblivious of this. We have removed your veil from you, and so today your eyesight is acute.’

22.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
22.text: (It will be said): 'Of this you have been heedless. Therefore, we have now removed your covering. Today your sight is sharp'

22.author: Saheeh International
22.text: [It will be said], "You were certainly in unmindfulness of this, and We have removed from you your cover, so your sight, this Day, is sharp."

22.author: Sarwar
22.text: (He will be told), "You were completely heedless of this day. We have removed the veil from your eyes and your vision will now be sharp and strong".

22.author: Shakir
22.text: Certainly you were heedless of it, but now We have removed from you your veil, so your sight today is sharp.

22.author: Wahiduddin Khan
22.text: You were heedless of this, but now We have removed your veil, so your sight today is sharp.

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: (It will be said:) "Thou wast heedless of this; now have We removed thy veil, and sharp is thy sight this Day!"

22.author: Transliteration
22.text: Laqad kunta fee ghaflatin min hatha fakashafna AAanka ghitaaka fabasaruka alyawma hadeedun

23.author: Ahmed Ali
23.text: His companion will say: "Here is (the record) I have ready with me."

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: And his accompanying angel said, “This is the record of your deeds, available with me.”

23.author: Arberry
23.text: And his comrade shall say, 'This is what I have, made ready.'

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: And his companion will say: this is that which with me is ready.

23.author: Hilali & Khan
23.text: And his companion (angel) will say: "Here is (this Record) ready with me!"

23.author: Itani
23.text: And His escort will say, “This is what I have ready with me.”

23.author: Maududi
23.text: His companion said: “Here is he who was in my charge.”

23.author: Mubarakpuri
23.text: And his companion (angel) will say: "Here is (his record) ready with me!"

23.author: Pickthall
23.text: And (unto the evil-doer) his comrade saith: This is that which I have ready (as testimony).

23.author: Qarai
23.text: Then his companion [angel] will say, ‘This is what is ready with me [of his record of deeds].’

23.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
23.text: And his companion will say: 'This is that which I have present'

23.author: Saheeh International
23.text: And his companion, [the angel], will say, "This [record] is what is with me, prepared."

23.author: Sarwar
23.text: His (angelic) companion will say, "(Lord), the record of his deeds is with me and is all ready".

23.author: Shakir
23.text: And his companions shall say: This is what is ready with me.

23.author: Wahiduddin Khan
23.text: His companion attendant will say, "I have here his record ready."

23.author: Yusuf Ali
23.text: And his Companion will say: "Here is (his Record) ready with me!"

23.author: Transliteration
23.text: Waqala qareenuhu hatha ma ladayya AAateedun

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: "Cast each stubborn unbeliever into Hell," (they will be told),

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: It will be said to the angels, “Both of you fling every excessive ungrateful, stubborn person into hell.”

24.author: Arberry
24.text: 'Cast, you twain, into Gehenna every froward unbeliever,

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: Cast ye twain into Hell every Person rebellious, contumacious.

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: (And it will be said): "Both of you throw (Order from Allah to the two angels) into Hell, every stubborn disbeliever (in the Oneness of Allah, in His Messengers, etc.).

24.author: Maududi
24.text: The command was given: “Cast into Hell every hardened, stubborn unbeliever,

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: (Allah will say to the angels:) "Both of you throw into Hell every stubborn disbeliever"

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: (And it is said): Do ye twain hurl to hell each rebel ingrate,

24.author: Qarai
24.text: [The two angels accompany him will be told,] ‘Cast every obdurate ingrate into hell,

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: (It will be said): 'Indeed, cast into Gehenna (Hell) every deviating unbeliever,

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: [Allah will say], "Throw into Hell every obstinate disbeliever,

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: (They will be told,) "Throw into hell every persistent disbelievers,

24.author: Shakir
24.text: Do cast into hell every ungrateful, rebellious one,

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: "Cast into Hell every ungrateful, rebellious one,

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: (The sentence will be:) "Throw, throw into Hell every contumacious Rejecter (of Allah)!-

24.author: Transliteration
24.text: Alqiya fee jahannama kulla kaffarin AAaneedin

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: "Every obstructor of good, transgressor, and the sceptic,

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: “The one who excessively prevented from virtue, the transgressor, the doubtful.”

25.author: Arberry
25.text: every hinderer of the good, transgressor, disquieter,

25.author: Maududi
25.text: who hinders good, exceeds the limits, is immersed in doubts,

25.author: Qarai
25.text: [every] hinderer of good, transgressor, and skeptic,

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: forbidder of good, transgressor and doubter,

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: Preventer of good, aggressor, and doubter,

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: who is an opponent of good, a suspicious transgressor

25.author: Shakir
25.text: Forbidder of good, exceeder of limits, doubter,

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: hinderer of good, transgressor, causing others to doubt,

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: "Who forbade what was good, transgressed all bounds, cast doubts and suspicions;

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: Who had set up another god with God. Cast him into severe torment."

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: “The one who appointed another God along with Allah – so both of you fling him into the severe punishment.”

26.author: Arberry
26.text: who set up with God another god; therefore, you twain, cast him into the terrible chastisement.'

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: Who set up with Allah another god. So cast him ye twain into the torment severe.

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: "Who set up another ilah (god) with Allah, then (both of you) cast him in the severe torment."

26.author: Itani
26.text: Who fabricated another god with God; toss him into the intense agony.”

26.author: Maududi
26.text: and has set up another deity with Allah. Hurl him into the grievous torment.”

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: "Who set up another god with Allah. Then both of you cast him in the severe torment."

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: Who setteth up another god along with Allah. Do ye twain hurl him to the dreadful doom.

26.author: Qarai
26.text: who had set up another god along with Allah and cast him into the severe punishment.’

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: who has set up with Allah another god. Indeed, you two, cast him into the terrible punishment'

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: Who made [as equal] with Allah another deity; then throw him into the severe punishment."

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: and an idol worshipper. Throw him into severe torment".

26.author: Shakir
26.text: Who sets up another god with Allah, so do cast him into severe chastisement.

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: who has set up another god besides God: cast him into severe punishment" --

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: "Who set up another god beside Allah: Throw him into a severe penalty."

26.author: Transliteration
26.text: Allathee jaAAala maAAa Allahi ilahan akhara faalqiyahu fee alAAathabi alshshadeedi

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: His companion will say: "O Lord, I did not lead him to wickedness, but he was himself far astray."

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: His accompanying devil said, “Our Lord! I did not cause him to rebel, but he himself was in extreme error.”

27.author: Arberry
27.text: And his comrade shall say, 'Our Lord, I made him not insolent, but he was in far error.'

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: His companion will say: O our Lord! caused him not to transgress but he was himself in error far off.

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: His companion (Satan devil)] will say: "Our Lord! I did not push him to transgress, (in disbelief, oppression, and evil deeds) but he was himself in error far astray."

27.author: Itani
27.text: His escort will say, “Our Lord, I did not make him rebel, but he was far astray.”

27.author: Maududi
27.text: His companion said: “I did not incite him to rebel; he was far gone into error of his own accord.”

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: His companion (devil) will say: "Our Lord! I did not push him to transgression, but he was himself in error far astray."

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: His comrade saith: Our Lord! I did not cause him to rebel, but he was (himself) far gone in error.

27.author: Qarai
27.text: His companion [devil] will say, ‘Our Lord! I did not incite him to rebel [against You], but he was [himself] in extreme error.’

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: And his companion shall say: 'Our Lord, I did not make him insolent, he was far astray'

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: His [devil] companion will say, "Our Lord, I did not make him transgress, but he [himself] was in extreme error."

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: His satanic companion will say, "Our Lord, I did not mislead him, but he himself went astray".

27.author: Shakir
27.text: His companion will say: Our Lord! I did not lead him into inordinacy but he himself was in a great error.

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: and his associate [Satan] will say, "Lord, I did not make him transgress, he had already gone far astray himself."

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: His Companion will say: "Our Lord! I did not make him transgress, but he was (himself) far astray."

27.author: Transliteration
27.text: Qala qareenuhu rabbana ma atghaytuhu walakin kana fee dalalin baAAeedin

28.author: Ahmed Ali
28.text: "Do not argue in My presence. I had announced the promise of doom in advance.

28.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
28.text: He will say, “Do not dispute before Me – I had already warned you of the punishment.”

28.author: Arberry
28.text: He shall say, 'Dispute not before Me! For I sent you beforehand -- the threat.

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: Allah will say: wrangle not in My presence, and had already proferred Unto you the threat.

28.author: Hilali & Khan
28.text: Allah will say: "Dispute not in front of Me, I had already, in advance, sent you the threat.

28.author: Itani
28.text: He will say, “Do not feud in My presence—I had warned you in advance.

28.author: Maududi
28.text: (It was said): “Do not remonstrate in My presence. I had warned you.

28.author: Mubarakpuri
28.text: (Allah) will say: "Dispute not in front of Me, I had already in advance sent you the threat."

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: He saith: Contend not in My presence, when I had already proffered unto you the warning.

28.author: Qarai
28.text: He will say, ‘Do not wrangle in My presence, for I had already warned you in advance.

28.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
28.text: He (Allah) will say: 'Do not dispute before Me. I sent you a warning beforehand.

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: [Allah] will say, "Do not dispute before Me, while I had already presented to you the warning.

28.author: Sarwar
28.text: The Lord will say, "Do not argue in My presence; I had certainly sent you a warning.

28.author: Shakir
28.text: He will say: Do not quarrel in My presence, and indeed I gave you the threatening beforehand:

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: God will say, "Do not quarrel in My presence. I gave you the warning beforehand

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: He will say: "Dispute not with each other in My Presence: I had already in advance sent you Warning.

28.author: Transliteration
28.text: Qala la takhtasimoo ladayya waqad qaddamtu ilaykum bialwaAAeedi

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: There is no changing of My word, nor am I unjust to My creatures."

29.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
29.text: “With Me, the decree does not change, and nor do I oppress the bondmen.”

29.author: Arberry
29.text: The Word is not changed with Me; I wrong not My servants.'

29.author: Daryabadi
29.text: The Word will not be changed in My presence, nor am an oppressor at all Unto My bondmen.

29.author: Hilali & Khan
29.text: The Sentence that comes from Me cannot be changed, and I am not unjust (to the least) to the slaves."

29.author: Itani
29.text: The decree from Me will not be changed, and I am not unjust to the servants.”

29.author: Maududi
29.text: My Word is not changed; and never do I inflict the least wrong upon My servants.”

29.author: Mubarakpuri
29.text: "The sentence that comes from Me cannot be changed, and I am not unjust to the servants."

29.author: Pickthall
29.text: The sentence that cometh from Me cannot be changed, and I am in no wise a tyrant unto the slaves.

29.author: Qarai
29.text: The word [of judgement] is unalterable with Me, and I am not tyrannical to My servants.’

29.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
29.text: The Word cannot be changed with Me; I do not wrong My worshipers'

29.author: Saheeh International
29.text: The word will not be changed with Me, and never will I be unjust to the servants."

29.author: Sarwar
29.text: No word is to be exchanged in My presence. I am not unjust to My servants".

29.author: Shakir
29.text: My word shall not be changed, nor am I in the least unjust to the servants.

29.author: Wahiduddin Khan
29.text: and My word shall not be changed, nor am I unjust to My servants."

29.author: Yusuf Ali
29.text: "The Word changes not before Me, and I do not the least injustice to My Servants."

29.author: Transliteration
29.text: Ma yubaddalu alqawlu ladayya wama ana bithallamin lilAAabeedi

30.author: Ahmed Ali
30.text: We shall ask Hell that day: "Are you full?" It will answer: "Are there still more?"

30.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
30.text: The day when We will ask hell, “Are you filled up?” and it will answer, “Are there some more?”

30.author: Arberry
30.text: Upon the day We shall say unto Gehenna, 'Art thou filled?' And it shall say, 'Are there any more to come?'

30.author: Daryabadi
30.text: Mention the Day whereon We shall say Unto the Hell: art thou filled? and it will say: is there yet any addition?

30.author: Hilali & Khan
30.text: On the Day when We will say to Hell: "Are you filled?" It will say: "Are there any more (to come)?"

30.author: Itani
30.text: On the Day when We will say to Hell, “Are you full?” And it will say, “Are there any more?”

30.author: Maududi
30.text: On that Day We shall ask Hell: “Are you full?” And it will reply: “Are there any more?”

30.author: Mubarakpuri
30.text: On the Day when We will say to Hell: "Are you filled" It will say: "Are there any more"

30.author: Pickthall
30.text: On the day when We say unto hell: Art thou filled? and it saith: Can there be more to come?

30.author: Qarai
30.text: The day when We shall say to hell, ‘Are you full?’ It will say, ‘Is there any more?’

30.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
30.text: On that Day We shall ask Gehenna: 'Are you full' And it will answer: 'Are there any more'

30.author: Saheeh International
30.text: On the Day We will say to Hell, "Have you been filled?" and it will say, "Are there some more,"

30.author: Sarwar
30.text: On that day We shall ask hell, "Are you full?" It will say, "Are there any more?"

30.author: Shakir
30.text: On the day that We will say to hell: Are you filled up? And it will say: Are there any more?

30.author: Wahiduddin Khan
30.text: On that Day, We shall ask Hell, "Are you now full?" Hell will answer, "Are there any more?"

30.author: Yusuf Ali
30.text: One Day We will ask Hell, "Art thou filled to the full?" It will say, "Are there any more (to come)?"

30.author: Transliteration
30.text: Yawma naqoolu lijahannama hali imtalati wataqoolu hal min mazeedin

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: And Paradise will be brought near, not far from those who took heed for themselves and feared God.

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: And Paradise will be brought close to the pious, not far away from them.

31.author: Arberry
31.text: And Paradise shall be brought forward to the godfearing, not afar:

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: And brought nigh will be the Garden Unto the God-fearing, not far-off.

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: And Paradise will be brought near to the Muttaqun (pious - see V. 2:2) not far off.

31.author: Itani
31.text: And Paradise will be brought closer to the pious, not far away.

31.author: Maududi
31.text: And when Paradise shall be brought close to the God-fearing, and will no longer be far away,

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: And Paradise will be Uzlifat to those who had Taqwa, not far off.

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: And the Garden is brought nigh for those who kept from evil, no longer distant.

31.author: Qarai
31.text: And paradise will be brought near for the Godwary, it will not be distant [any more]:

31.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
31.text: And Paradise, which is not far away, shall be brought closer to those who were cautious.

31.author: Saheeh International
31.text: And Paradise will be brought near to the righteous, not far,

31.author: Sarwar
31.text: Paradise will be brought near for the pious ones

31.author: Shakir
31.text: And the garden shall be brought near to those who guard (against evil), not far off:

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: Paradise will be brought near to the righteous and will no longer be far away.

31.author: Yusuf Ali
31.text: And the Garden will be brought nigh to the Righteous,- no more a thing distant.

32.author: Ahmed Ali
32.text: "This is what you had been promised," (will be said) to every penitent who remembered (his duty),

32.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
32.text: This is what you are promised – for every repenting, careful person.

32.author: Arberry
32.text: 'This is that you were promised; it is for every mindful penitent.'

32.author: Daryabadi
32.text: This is that which ye were promised: for every oft- returning heedful one.

32.author: Hilali & Khan
32.text: (It will be said): "This is what you were promised, - (it is) for those oft-returning (to Allah) in sincere repentance, and those who preserve their covenant with Allah (by obeying Him in all what He has ordered, and worship none but Allah Alone, i.e. follow Allah's Religion, Islamic Monotheism).

32.author: Itani
32.text: “This is what you were promised—for every careful penitent.

32.author: Maududi
32.text: it will be said: “This is what you were promised, a promise made to everyone who turned much (to Allah) and was watchful of his conduct,

32.author: Mubarakpuri
32.text: (It will be said): "This is what you were promised -- (it is) for those returning in sincere repentance, and Hafiz."

32.author: Pickthall
32.text: (And it is said): This is that which ye were promised. (It is) for every penitent and heedful one,

32.author: Qarai
32.text: ‘This is what you were promised. [It is] for every penitent and dutiful [servant]

32.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
32.text: (It shall be said to them): 'This is that you were promised. It is for every heeding penitent.

32.author: Saheeh International
32.text: [It will be said], "This is what you were promised - for every returner [to Allah] and keeper [of His covenant]

32.author: Sarwar
32.text: (and they will be told), "This is what you were promised. It is for everyone who turned in repentance to God, kept his promise,

32.author: Shakir
32.text: This is what you were promised, (it is) for every one who turns frequently (to Allah), keeps (His limits);

32.author: Wahiduddin Khan
32.text: This is what you were promised -- this is for everyone who often turned to God and kept Him in mind,

32.author: Yusuf Ali
32.text: (A voice will say:) "This is what was promised for you,- for every one who turned (to Allah) in sincere repentance, who kept (His Law),

32.author: Transliteration
32.text: Hatha ma tooAAadoona likulli awwabin hafeethin

33.author: Ahmed Ali
33.text: Who feared Ar-Rahman in secret, and came with a penitent heart:

33.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
33.text: Who fears the Most Gracious without seeing, and came with a heart that inclines.

33.author: Arberry
33.text: Whosoever fears the All-merciful in the Unseen, and comes with a penitent heart:

33.author: Daryabadi
33.text: That feareth the Compassionate in the unseen and cometh to Him with a heart penitent:

33.author: Hilali & Khan
33.text: "Who feared the Most Beneficent (Allah) in the Ghaib (unseen): (i.e. in this worldly life before seeing and meeting Him), and brought a heart turned in repentance (to Him - and absolutely free from each and every kind of polytheism),

33.author: Itani
33.text: Who inwardly feared the Most Gracious, and came with a repentant heart.

33.author: Maududi
33.text: to everyone who feared the Merciful One though He is beyond the reach of perception, to everyone who has come with a heart ever wont to turn (to Him).

33.author: Mubarakpuri
33.text: "Who feared Ar-Rahman unseen and brought a heart turned in repentance."

33.author: Pickthall
33.text: Who feareth the Beneficent in secret and cometh with a contrite heart.

33.author: Qarai
33.text: who fears the All-beneficent in secret and comes with a penitent heart.

33.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
33.text: Whosoever fears the Merciful in the Unseen, and comes with a contrite heart.

33.author: Saheeh International
33.text: Who feared the Most Merciful unseen and came with a heart returning [in repentance].

33.author: Sarwar
33.text: feared the Beneficent God in secret, and turned to Him with a repenting heart".

33.author: Shakir
33.text: Who fears the Beneficent Allah in secret and comes with a penitent heart:

33.author: Wahiduddin Khan
33.text: who fears the Compassionate One, though He is unseen, and comes to Him with a penitent heart;

33.author: Yusuf Ali
33.text: "Who feared (Allah) Most Gracious Unseen, and brought a heart turned in devotion (to Him):

33.author: Transliteration
33.text: Man khashiya alrrahmana bialghaybi wajaa biqalbin muneebin

34.author: Ahmed Ali
34.text: "Enter it in peace. This is the day of life abiding."

34.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
34.text: It will be said to him, “Enter it in peace; this is the day of eternity.”

34.author: Arberry
34.text: 'Enter it in peace! This is the Day of Eternity.'

34.author: Daryabadi
34.text: Enter it in peace. This is the Day of Abidence.

34.author: Hilali & Khan
34.text: "Enter you therein in peace and security; this is a Day of eternal life!"

34.author: Itani
34.text: Enter it in peace. This is the Day of Eternity.”

34.author: Maududi
34.text: Enter this Paradise in peace.” That will be the Day of Eternity.

34.author: Mubarakpuri
34.text: "Enter you therein in peace and security -- this is a Day of eternal life!"

34.author: Pickthall
34.text: Enter it in peace. This is the day of immortality.

34.author: Qarai
34.text: Enter it in peace! This is the day of immortality.’

34.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
34.text: Enter it in peace. This is the Day of Eternity'

34.author: Saheeh International
34.text: Enter it in peace. This is the Day of Eternity."

34.author: Sarwar
34.text: (They will be told), "Enter Paradise in peace and, therein, you will live forever".

34.author: Shakir
34.text: Enter it in peace, that is the day of abiding.

34.author: Wahiduddin Khan
34.text: so enter it in peace. This is the Day of everlasting life.

34.author: Yusuf Ali
34.text: "Enter ye therein in Peace and Security; this is a Day of Eternal Life!"

35.author: Ahmed Ali
35.text: Theirs will be whatsoever they wish: And with Us there is more.

35.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
35.text: In it for them is all that they may desire, and with Us is more than it.

35.author: Arberry
35.text: Therein they shall have whatever they will; and with Us there is yet more.

35.author: Daryabadi
35.text: Theirs therein will be whatsoever they list; and with Us will be yet more.

35.author: Hilali & Khan
35.text: There they will have all that they desire, and We have more (for them, i.e. a glance at the All-Mighty, All-Majestic).

35.author: Itani
35.text: Therein they will have whatever they desire—and We have even more.

35.author: Maududi
35.text: Therein they shall get whatever they desire; and with Us there is even more.

35.author: Mubarakpuri
35.text: There they will have all that they desire -- and We have more.

35.author: Pickthall
35.text: There they have all that they desire, and there is more with Us.

35.author: Qarai
35.text: There they will have whatever they wish, and with Us there is yet more.

35.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
35.text: There they have all that they desire, and with Us is much more.

35.author: Saheeh International
35.text: They will have whatever they wish therein, and with Us is more.

35.author: Sarwar
35.text: They will have therein whatever they want and will receive from Us more rewards

35.author: Shakir
35.text: They have therein what they wish and with Us is more yet.

35.author: Wahiduddin Khan
35.text: There they shall have all that they desire, and there is even more with Us.

35.author: Yusuf Ali
35.text: There will be for them therein all that they wish,- and more besides in Our Presence.

35.author: Transliteration
35.text: Lahum ma yashaoona feeha waladayna mazeedun

36.author: Ahmed Ali
36.text: How many generations have We destroyed before them who were mightier in power than they. Then they searched throughout the land to see if there was a way of escape.

36.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
36.text: And many a generation We did destroy before them, who exceeded them in strength, therefore venturing in the cities! Is there a place to escape?

36.author: Arberry
36.text: How many a generation We destroyed before them that was stronger in valour than they, then they searched about in the land; was there any asylum?

36.author: Daryabadi
36.text: And how many a generation have We destroyed before them, who were mightier in power than they, and they traversed the cities! No place of refuge could they find.

36.author: Hilali & Khan
36.text: And how many a generation We have destroyed before them, who were stronger in power than them, and (when Our Torment came) they ran for a refuge in the land! Could they find any place of refuge (for them to save themselves from destruction)?

36.author: Itani
36.text: How many generations before them, who were more powerful than they, did We destroy? They explored the lands—was there any escape?

36.author: Maududi
36.text: How many a nation did We destroy before them that were stronger in prowess than these. They searched about the lands of the world. But could they find a refuge?

36.author: Mubarakpuri
36.text: And how many a generation We have destroyed before them who were stronger in power than they. And they went about the land! Could they find any place of refuge

36.author: Pickthall
36.text: And how many a generation We destroyed before them, who were mightier than these in prowess so that they overran the lands! Had they any place of refuge (when the judgment came)?

36.author: Qarai
36.text: How many generations We have destroyed before them, who were stronger than these, insomuch that they ransacked the towns?! So, is there any escape [from Allah’s punishment]?

36.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
36.text: How many generations, stronger in might than they have We destroyed before them! They searched the land, could they find any asylum?

36.author: Saheeh International
36.text: And how many a generation before them did We destroy who were greater than them in [striking] power and had explored throughout the lands. Is there any place of escape?

36.author: Sarwar
36.text: How many an ancient town who were much stronger than them (unbelievers) did We destroy. (In vain), they wandered through the land in search of a place of refuge from Our torment.

36.author: Shakir
36.text: And how many a generation did We destroy before them who were mightier in prowess than they, so they went about and about in the lands. Is there a place of refuge?

36.author: Wahiduddin Khan
36.text: How many a generation, far greater in prowess, have We destroyed before them! They searched the entire land: but could they find a refuge?

36.author: Yusuf Ali
36.text: But how many generations before them did We destroy (for their sins),- stronger in power than they? Then did they wander through the land: was there any place of escape (for them)?

36.author: Transliteration
36.text: Wakam ahlakna qablahum min qarnin hum ashaddu minhum batshan fanaqqaboo fee albiladi hal min maheesin

37.author: Ahmed Ali
37.text: Surely there is a reminder in this for whosoever has a heart (to feel), or takes heed.

37.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
37.text: Indeed in this is an advice for anyone who keeps a heart, or listens attentively.

37.author: Arberry
37.text: Surely in that there is a reminder to him who has a heart, or will give ear with a present mind.

37.author: Daryabadi
37.text: Verily herein is an admonition Unto him who hath a heart, or giveth ear while he is heedful.

37.author: Hilali & Khan
37.text: Verily, therein is indeed a reminder for him who has a heart or gives ear while he is heedful.

37.author: Itani
37.text: In that is a reminder for whoever possesses a heart, or cares to listen and witness.

37.author: Maududi
37.text: Verily there is a lesson in this for everyone who has a (sound) heart and who listens with an attentive mind.

37.author: Mubarakpuri
37.text: Verily, therein is indeed a reminder for him who has a heart or gives ear while he is heedful.

37.author: Pickthall
37.text: Lo! therein verily is a reminder for him who hath a heart, or giveth ear with full intelligence.

37.author: Qarai
37.text: There is indeed an admonition in that for one who has a heart, or gives ear, being attentive.

37.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
37.text: Surely, in this there is a Reminder for he who has a heart or listens attentively while witnessing.

37.author: Saheeh International
37.text: Indeed in that is a reminder for whoever has a heart or who listens while he is present [in mind].

37.author: Sarwar
37.text: This is a reminder for everyone who understands, listens, and sees.

37.author: Shakir
37.text: Most surely there is a reminder in this for him who has a heart or he gives ear and is a witness.

37.author: Wahiduddin Khan
37.text: There is truly a reminder in this for whoever has a heart, whoever listens attentively.

37.author: Yusuf Ali
37.text: Verily in this is a Message for any that has a heart and understanding or who gives ear and earnestly witnesses (the truth).

37.author: Transliteration
37.text: Inna fee thalika lathikra liman kana lahu qalbun aw alqa alssamAAa wahuwa shaheedun

38.author: Ahmed Ali
38.text: We created the heavens and the earth and all that lies between them, in six spans, and no weariness came upon Us.

38.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
38.text: And We have indeed created the heavens and the earth, and all what is between them, in six days; and fatigue did not even approach Us.

38.author: Arberry
38.text: We created the heavens and the earth, and what between them is, in six days, and no weariness touched Us.

38.author: Daryabadi
38.text: And assuredly We created the heavens and the earth and whatsoever is in between the twain in six days, and there touched Us naught of weariness.

38.author: Hilali & Khan
38.text: And indeed We created the heavens and the earth and all between them in six Days and nothing of fatigue touched Us.

38.author: Itani
38.text: We created the heavens and the earth and what is between them in six days, and no fatigue touched Us.

38.author: Maududi
38.text: We created the heavens and the earth and all that is between them in six days, and weariness did not even touch Us.

38.author: Mubarakpuri
38.text: And indeed We created the heavens and the earth and all between them in six Days and nothing of fatigue touched Us.

38.author: Pickthall
38.text: And verily We created the heavens and the earth, and all that is between them, in six Days, and naught of weariness touched Us.

38.author: Qarai
38.text: Certainly We created the heavens and the earth, and whatever is between them, in six days, and any fatigue did not touch Us.

38.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
38.text: In six days We created the heavens and the earth and all that is between them and no weariness touched Us!

38.author: Saheeh International
38.text: And We did certainly create the heavens and earth and what is between them in six days, and there touched Us no weariness.

38.author: Sarwar
38.text: We created the heavens, the earth, and all that is between them in six days without experiencing any fatigue.

38.author: Shakir
38.text: And certainly We created the heavens and the earth and what is between them in six periods and there touched Us not any fatigue.

38.author: Wahiduddin Khan
38.text: We created the heavens, the earth, and everything between them in six days [periods] nor were We ever wearied.

38.author: Yusuf Ali
38.text: We created the heavens and the earth and all between them in Six Days, nor did any sense of weariness touch Us.

38.author: Transliteration
38.text: Walaqad khalaqna alssamawati waalarda wama baynahuma fee sittati ayyamin wama massana min lughoobin

39.author: Ahmed Ali
39.text: So you bear with patience what they say, and sing the praises of your Lord before the rising of the sun and its setting,

39.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
39.text: Therefore (O dear prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) patiently bear upon what they say, and praising your Lord say His Purity before the sun rises and before the sun sets.

39.author: Arberry
39.text: So be thou patient under what they say, and proclaim thy Lord's praise before the rising of the sun, and before its setting,

39.author: Daryabadi
39.text: So bear thou patiently with that which they say, and hallow the praise of thine Lord before the rising of the sun and before its setting.

39.author: Hilali & Khan
39.text: So bear with patience (O Muhammad SAW) all that they say, and glorify the Praises of your Lord, before the rising of the sun and before (its) setting (i.e. the Fajr, Zuhr, and 'Asr prayers).

39.author: Itani
39.text: So endure what they say, and proclaim the praises of your Lord before the rising of the sun, and before sunset.

39.author: Maududi
39.text: Hence bear with patience whatever they say, and celebrate your Lord's glory before the rising of the sun and before its setting;

39.author: Mubarakpuri
39.text: So bear with patience all that they say, and glorify the praises of your Lord, before the rising of the sun and before (its) setting.

39.author: Pickthall
39.text: Therefor (O Muhammad) bear with what they say, and hymn the praise of thy Lord before the rising and before the setting of the sun;

39.author: Qarai
39.text: So be patient at what they say, and celebrate the praise of your Lord before the rising of the sun and before the sunset,

39.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
39.text: Bear then with patience what they say. Exalt with the praise of your Lord before sunrise and before sunset.

39.author: Saheeh International
39.text: So be patient, [O Muhammad], over what they say and exalt [Allah] with praise of your Lord before the rising of the sun and before its setting,

39.author: Sarwar
39.text: (Muhammad), exercise patience against what they say. Glorify your Lord with His praise before sunrise and sunset.

39.author: Shakir
39.text: Therefore be patient of what they say, and sing the praise of your Lord before the rising of the sun and before the setting.

39.author: Wahiduddin Khan
