39.text: So bear with patience what they say, and glorify your Lord with His praise, before the rising and before the setting of the sun;

39.author: Yusuf Ali
39.text: Bear, then, with patience, all that they say, and celebrate the praises of thy Lord, before the rising of the sun and before (its) setting.

39.author: Transliteration
39.text: Faisbir AAala ma yaqooloona wasabbih bihamdi rabbika qabla tulooAAi alshshamsi waqabla alghuroobi

40.author: Ahmed Ali
40.text: And glorify Him for some portion of the night, and additional adorations.

40.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
40.text: And say His Purity during the night, and after the customary prayers.

40.author: Arberry
40.text: and proclaim thy Lord's praise in the night, and at the ends of the prostrations.

40.author: Daryabadi
40.text: And in the night-time hallow Him, and also after the prescribed prostration.

40.author: Hilali & Khan
40.text: And during a part of the night (also), glorify His praises (i.e. Maghrib and 'Isha prayers), and (so likewise) after the prayers [As-Sunnah, Nawafil optional and additional prayers, and also glorify, praise and magnify Allah - Subhan Allah, Alhamdu lillah, Allahu-Akbar].

40.author: Itani
40.text: And glorify Him during the night, and at the end of devotions.

40.author: Maududi
40.text: and in the night, too, celebrate His glory, in the wake of prostration.

40.author: Mubarakpuri
40.text: And during a part of the night (also) glorify His praises and (so likewise) after the prostrations.

40.author: Pickthall
40.text: And in the night-time hymn His praise, and after the (prescribed) prostrations.

40.author: Qarai
40.text: and glorify Him through part of the night and after the prostrations.

40.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
40.text: And exalt Him in the night, and at the ends of the prostrations.

40.author: Saheeh International
40.text: And [in part] of the night exalt Him and after prostration.

40.author: Sarwar
40.text: Glorify Him during the night and also glorify Him after prostration.

40.author: Shakir
40.text: And glorify Him in the night and after the prayers.

40.author: Wahiduddin Khan
40.text: proclaim His praise in the night and at the end of every prayer.

40.author: Yusuf Ali
40.text: And during part of the night, (also,) celebrate His praises, and (so likewise) after the postures of adoration.

41.author: Ahmed Ali
41.text: Take heed that the day the crier calls from a place close (to every one),

41.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
41.text: And listen attentively on the day when an announcer will proclaim from a place nearby.

41.author: Arberry
41.text: And listen thou for the day when the caller shall call from a near place.

41.author: Daryabadi
41.text: And hearken thou: the Day whereon the caller will call from a place quite near.

41.author: Hilali & Khan
41.text: And listen on the Day when the caller will call from a near place,

41.author: Itani
41.text: And listen for the Day when the caller calls from a nearby place.

41.author: Maududi
41.text: Hearken on the Day when the caller will call from a place nearby,

41.author: Mubarakpuri
41.text: And listen on the Day when the caller will call from a near place.

41.author: Pickthall
41.text: And listen on the day when the crier crieth from a near place,

41.author: Qarai
41.text: And be on the alert for the day when the caller calls from a close quarter,

41.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
41.text: Listen for the Day when the Caller will call from a nearby place.

41.author: Saheeh International
41.text: And listen on the Day when the Caller will call out from a place that is near -

41.author: Sarwar
41.text: Wait for the day when the trumpet will be sounded from a nearby place.

41.author: Shakir
41.text: And listen on the day when the crier shall cry from a near place

41.author: Wahiduddin Khan
41.text: Hearken! The Day when the caller will call from near.

41.author: Yusuf Ali
41.text: And listen for the Day when the Caller will call out from a place quiet near,-

41.author: Transliteration
41.text: WaistamiAA yawma yunadi almunadi min makanin qareebin

42.author: Ahmed Ali
42.text: The day they actually hear the blast, will be the Day of rising of the dead.

42.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
42.text: The day when all will hear the Scream with the truth; this is the day of coming out of the graves.

42.author: Arberry
42.text: On the day they hear the Cry in truth, that is the day of coming forth.

42.author: Daryabadi
42.text: The Day whereon they will surely hear the shout - that is the Day of coming forth.

42.author: Hilali & Khan
42.text: The Day when they will hear As-Saihah (shout, etc.) in truth, that will be the Day of coming out (from the graves i.e. the Day of Resurrection).

42.author: Itani
42.text: The Day when they will hear the Shout in all truth. That is the Day of Emergence.

42.author: Maududi
42.text: the Day when they shall hear the Blast in truth. That will be the Day (for the dead) to come forth.

42.author: Mubarakpuri
42.text: The Day when they will hear As-Sayhah in truth, that will be the Day of coming out.

42.author: Pickthall
42.text: The day when they will hear the (Awful) Cry in truth. That is the day of coming forth (from the graves).

42.author: Qarai
42.text: the day when they hear the Cry in all truth. That is the day of rising [from the dead].

42.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
42.text: On the Day when they will hear the Shout, in truth that Day they will emerge.

42.author: Saheeh International
42.text: The Day they will hear the blast [of the Horn] in truth. That is the Day of Emergence [from the graves].

42.author: Sarwar
42.text: On that day they will certainly hear the sound of the trumpet and that will be the Day of Resurrection.

42.author: Shakir
42.text: The day when they shall hear the cry in truth; that is the day of coming forth.

42.author: Wahiduddin Khan
42.text: The Day when men will hear the fateful cry, they will rise up [from their graves].

42.author: Yusuf Ali
42.text: The Day when they will hear a (mighty) Blast in (very) truth: that will be the Day of Resurrection.

42.author: Transliteration
42.text: Yawma yasmaAAoona alssayhata bialhaqqi thalika yawmu alkhurooji

43.author: Ahmed Ali
43.text: We are the one who give life and death, and to Us will be the destination.

43.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
43.text: Indeed it is We Who give life, and give death, and towards Us only is the return.

43.author: Arberry
43.text: It is We who give life, and make to die, and to Us is the homecoming.

43.author: Daryabadi
43.text: Verily We! it is We Who give life and cause death, and Unto Us is the journeying.

43.author: Hilali & Khan
43.text: Verily, We it is Who give life and cause death; and to Us is the final return,

43.author: Itani
43.text: It is We who control life and death, and to Us is the destination.

43.author: Maududi
43.text: Surely it is We Who give life and cause death, and to Us shall all return

43.author: Mubarakpuri
43.text: Verily, it is We Who give life and cause death; and to Us is the final return.

43.author: Pickthall
43.text: Lo! We it is Who quicken and give death, and unto Us is the journeying.

43.author: Qarai
43.text: Indeed it is We who give life and bring death, and toward Us is the [final] destination.

43.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
43.text: It is We who give life and make to die. To Us is the arrival.

43.author: Saheeh International
43.text: Indeed, it is We who give life and cause death, and to Us is the destination

43.author: Sarwar
43.text: We give life and cause things to die. To Us all things will return.

43.author: Shakir
43.text: Surely We give life and cause to die, and to Us is the eventual coming;

43.author: Wahiduddin Khan
43.text: Truly, it is We who give life and cause death, and to Us shall all return

43.author: Yusuf Ali
43.text: Verily it is We Who give Life and Death; and to Us is the Final Goal-

43.author: Transliteration
43.text: Inna nahnu nuhyee wanumeetu wailayna almaseeru

44.author: Ahmed Ali
44.text: The day the earth will split asunder they will come out hurriedly. This gathering together is easy for Us.

44.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
44.text: On the day when the earth will split for them, so they will come out in haste; this is the gathering – easy for Us.

44.author: Arberry
44.text: Upon the day when the earth is split asunder from about them as they hasten forth; that is a mustering easy for Us.

44.author: Daryabadi
44.text: That shall be the Day whereon the earth will be cleft from off them as they hasten forth. That shall be a gathering Unto Us easy.

44.author: Hilali & Khan
44.text: On the Day when the earth shall be cleft, from off them, (they will come out) hastening forth. That will be a gathering, quite easy for Us.

44.author: Itani
44.text: The Day when the earth will crack for them at once. That is an easy gathering for Us.

44.author: Maududi
44.text: on the Day when the earth will be rent asunder and people, rising from it, will hasten forth. To assemble them all will be easy for Us.

44.author: Mubarakpuri
44.text: On the Day when the earth shall be cleft, from off them (they will come out) hastening forth. That will be a gathering, quite easy for Us.

44.author: Pickthall
44.text: On the day when the earth splitteth asunder from them, hastening forth (they come). That is a gathering easy for Us (to make).

44.author: Qarai
44.text: The day the earth is split open for [disentombing] them, [they will come out] hastening. That mustering is easy for Us [to carry out].

44.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
44.text: Upon that Day the earth will be rent asunder from around them as they hurry forth that is an easy gathering for Us.

44.author: Saheeh International
44.text: On the Day the earth breaks away from them [and they emerge] rapidly; that is a gathering easy for Us.

44.author: Sarwar
44.text: On the day when the earth is rent asunder, they will quickly come out of their graves. This is how easy it is for Us to bring about the Day of Resurrection.

44.author: Shakir
44.text: The day on which the earth shall cleave asunder under them, they will make haste; that is a gathering together easy to Us.

44.author: Wahiduddin Khan
44.text: on the Day the earth will be rent asunder over them, and from it they shall emerge in haste. To assemble them all is easy enough  for Us.

44.author: Yusuf Ali
44.text: The Day when the Earth will be rent asunder, from (men) hurrying out: that will be a gathering together,- quite easy for Us.

44.author: Transliteration
44.text: Yawma tashaqqaqu alardu AAanhum siraAAan thalika hashrun AAalayna yaseerun

45.author: Ahmed Ali
45.text: We are cognisant of what they say; but it is not for you to compel them. So keep on reminding through the Qur'an whoever fears My warning.

45.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
45.text: We well know what they say (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), and you are not one to use force over them; therefore advise him with the Qur’an, whoever fears My threat.

45.author: Arberry
45.text: We know very well what they say; thou art not a tyrant over them. Therefore remind by the Koran him who fears My threat.

45.author: Daryabadi
45.text: We are the best Knower of that which they say, and thou art not over them over them tyrant. Wherefore admonish thou by the Qur'an him who feareth My threat.

45.author: Hilali & Khan
45.text: We know of best what they say; and you (O Muhammad SAW) are not a tyrant over them (to force them to Belief). But warn by the Quran, him who fears My Threat.

45.author: Itani
45.text: We are fully aware of what they say, and you are not a dictator over them. So remind by the Quran whoever fears My warning.

45.author: Maududi
45.text: (O Prophet), We are well aware of what they say; and you are not required to force things on them. So exhort with the Qur'an all those who fear My warning.

45.author: Mubarakpuri
45.text: We know best what they say. And you are not the one to force them. But warn by the Qur'an; him who fears My threat.

45.author: Pickthall
45.text: We are Best Aware of what they say, and thou (O Muhammad) art in no wise a compeller over them. But warn by the Qur'an him who feareth My threat.

45.author: Qarai
45.text: We know well what they say, and you are not there to compel them. So admonish by the Quran those who fear My threat.

45.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
45.text: Indeed, We know what they say. You (Prophet Muhammad) are not a tyrant over them. Therefore, remind by the Koran whosoever fears (My) threat.

45.author: Saheeh International
45.text: We are most knowing of what they say, and you are not over them a tyrant. But remind by the Qur'an whoever fears My threat.

45.author: Sarwar
45.text: We know best what they say and you cannot compel them. Remind, by way of the Quran, those who have fear of My warnings.

45.author: Shakir
45.text: We know best what they say, and you are not one to compel them; therefore remind him by means of the Quran who fears My threat.

45.author: Wahiduddin Khan
45.text: We know best what those who deny the truth say. You are not there to force them: so remind, with this Quran, those who fear My warning.

45.author: Yusuf Ali
45.text: We know best what they say; and thou art not one to overawe them by force. So admonish with the Qur'an such as fear My Warning!

45.author: Transliteration
45.text: Nahnu aAAlamu bima yaqooloona wama anta AAalayhim bijabbarin fathakkir bialqurani man yakhafu waAAeedi
